file_path <- "../data/lgg-mrna-expression-data-with-cancer-subtypes.csv"

lgg_data <- read.csv(file_path)

## 4 104.9960 299.662 810.295 0.0000  68.1495  2.2825 278.141 0.6521 162.7110
## 5  23.2001 191.149 444.332 0.0000  47.4089  2.5218 169.966 0.0000 111.4610
##    ZSWIM4  ZSWIM5  ZSWIM6  ZSWIM7    ZUFSP    ZW10   ZWILCH     ZWINT     ZXDA
## 1 237.466 388.085 615.901 480.260 162.7860 276.065  80.1636   50.3461  64.1913
## 2 324.017 371.981 500.000 264.727 153.2090 301.242 187.2980  110.4210 105.9350
## 3 326.644 465.744 869.204 492.841 150.8650 320.415 120.0310  186.5050 115.2250
## 4 442.483 377.268 743.450 306.588 224.6650 436.940 667.4480 1183.6500  89.0183
## 5 170.470 341.949 611.272 527.475  98.8526 272.853  57.3244   56.4872  42.3654
##      ZXDB    ZXDC ZYG11A  ZYG11B     ZYX    ZZEF1     ZZZ3
## 1 523.180 1311.52 0.0000 1495.70 1598.91 1171.810  836.165
## 2 467.564 1226.71 0.3451 1751.21 3889.92 1707.730 1015.530
## 3 454.325 1102.42 0.3460 1586.16 2214.53 1423.880  706.920
## 4 491.068 1288.32 3.2607 1760.15 2131.55 1916.990  689.321
## 5 323.793 1012.73 0.5044 1225.07 1692.60  729.794  456.941
##  [ reached 'max' / getOption("max.print") -- omitted 5 rows ]
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
erbb2_summary <- lgg_data %>%
  group_by(SUBTYPE) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_expression = mean(ERBB2, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_expression = median(ERBB2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_expression = sd(ERBB2, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_expression = min(ERBB2, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_expression = max(ERBB2, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

erbb2_summary
## # A tibble: 3 × 7
##   SUBTYPE       n mean_expression median_expression sd_expression min_expression
##   <chr>     <int>           <dbl>             <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1 LGG_IDHm…   167            699.              670.          188.           330.
## 2 LGG_IDHm…   248            597.              585.          158.           217.
## 3 LGG_IDHwt    92           1586.             1495.         1055.           127.
## # ℹ 1 more variable: max_expression <dbl>
ggplot(lgg_data, aes(x = SUBTYPE)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#ffc238", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    stat = "count",
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    vjust = -0.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Frequency of LGG Subtypes",
    x = "LGG Subtype",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.6, face = "bold")
  )

ggplot(lgg_data, aes(x = SUBTYPE, y = ERBB2)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#ffc238", width = 0.6, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(
    title = "ERBB2 Expression by Subtype",
    x = "LGG Subtype",
    y = "ERBB2 Expression"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 20)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)
  )

ggplot(lgg_data, aes(x = SUBTYPE, y = ERBB2)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightyellow") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white")

# Perform One-way ANOVA to test if ERBB2 expression differs across subtypes
anova_result <- aov(ERBB2 ~ SUBTYPE, data = lgg_data)

# Display the ANOVA table to check for statistical significance (p-value)
summary(anova_result)
##              Df    Sum Sq  Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)    
## SUBTYPE       2  68623247 34311624   152.6 <2e-16 ***
## Residuals   504 113320688   224843                   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# Interpretation: There is a statistically significant difference in ERBB2 expression levels between at least two of the LGG tumor subtypes.
ggplot(lgg_data, aes(x = ERBB2)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Raw ERBB2 Expression")

lgg_data$ERBB2_log2 <- log2(lgg_data$ERBB2 + 1)

ggplot(lgg_data, aes(x = log2(ERBB2 + 1))) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "darkorange", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Log2(ERBB2 + 1) Expression")

# Visualizing the Impact of Log Transformations
lgg_comparison <- lgg_data %>%
  mutate(
    raw = ERBB2,
    log2_trans = log2(ERBB2 + 1),
    ln_trans = log(ERBB2 + 1),
    log10_trans = log10(ERBB2 + 1)
  )

plot_hist <- function(data, col, title, color) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[col]])) +  
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = color, color = "white") +
    labs(title = title, x = "Expression Value", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal()
}


p1 <- plot_hist(lgg_comparison, "raw", "Original (Raw) ERBB2", "gray")
p2 <- plot_hist(lgg_comparison, "log2_trans", "log2(x + 1)", "steelblue")
p3 <- plot_hist(lgg_comparison, "ln_trans", "ln(x + 1)", "darkgreen")
p4 <- plot_hist(lgg_comparison, "log10_trans", "log10(x + 1)", "firebrick")


(p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)

# Identify all gene expression columns (excluding SUBTYPE)
gene_cols <- sapply(lgg_data, is.numeric)
gene_cols["SUBTYPE"] <- FALSE

# Apply log2(x + 1) transformation
lgg_data_log2 <- lgg_data
lgg_data_log2[gene_cols] <- log2(lgg_data_log2[gene_cols] + 1)

# Print first 10 rows and default number of columns
head(lgg_data_log2, 10)

## 1  9.187147 8.279049  9.146072 11.28980 9.277699 6.938639 9.210834 0.0000000

## 2 8.682777 8.272135  9.806960 0.0000000 1.025171 6.985751 0.7571086  9.694901

## 5 8.612378 7.288543  9.800385 0.0000000 1.006262 7.975344 0.0000000 10.564197

## 3 10.29382 5.667523 7.521200 0.0000000 5.637752 7.026933  4.709434 6.719129
## 4 10.40526 6.455094 7.290360 0.4071896 5.302933 7.171397  4.232691 6.740982
## 5 10.10722 5.182076 7.659896 0.0000000 5.989580 6.202151  7.130056 6.191537
##    ZSCAN18  ZSCAN20  ZSCAN21  ZSCAN22  ZSCAN23   ZSCAN2  ZSCAN29    ZSCAN4
## 1 11.79035 5.880147 8.781776 6.831105 6.327242 7.983849 9.239155 0.8789999
## 2 12.08400 6.644344 8.565955 7.007577 6.644344 7.328810 9.290616 0.7571086
## 3 10.52180 6.399017 8.391828 5.706285 8.059312 7.772460 9.457537 0.0000000
## 4 10.75915 6.741116 8.150179 6.621996 6.727866 8.231999 9.664083 0.0000000
## 5 10.87745 5.369746 8.265643 5.438473 4.596941 7.586082 8.798737 0.0000000
##    ZSCAN5A  ZSCAN5B   ZSWIM1   ZSWIM2   ZSWIM3   ZSWIM4   ZSWIM5   ZSWIM6
## 1 6.193038 1.977060 8.453213 1.977060 7.001397 7.897640 8.603942 9.268895
## 2 7.115626 1.772097 7.750111 2.543719 6.340833 8.344371 8.542958 8.968667
## 3 5.208915 1.253445 7.548121 2.458986 7.019257 8.355985 8.866488 9.765210
## 4 6.111647 1.714795 8.124850 0.724301 7.355007 8.792735 8.563265 9.540031
## 5 5.597200 1.816313 7.417566 0.000000 6.813281 7.421812 8.421850 9.258029
##     ZSWIM7    ZUFSP     ZW10   ZWILCH     ZWINT     ZXDA     ZXDB      ZXDC
## 1 8.910673 7.355668 8.114081 6.342761  5.682183 6.026608 9.033918 10.358124
## 2 8.053801 7.268743 8.239560 7.556874  6.799877 6.740590 8.872102 10.261754
## 3 8.947903 7.246646 8.328293 6.919233  7.550785 6.860777 8.830753 10.107766
## 4 8.264855 7.818039 8.774589 9.384672 10.210245 6.492146 8.942714 10.332395
## 5 9.045691 6.641728 8.097258 5.866028  5.845169 5.438473 8.343377  9.985458
##      ZYG11A   ZYG11B      ZYX     ZZEF1     ZZZ3 ERBB2_log2
## 1 0.0000000 10.54757 10.64378 10.195754 9.709368   3.337139
## 2 0.4277134 10.77496 11.92590 10.738709 9.989437   3.524471
## 3 0.4286784 10.63223 11.11344 10.476625 9.467443   3.336933
## 4 2.0910905 10.78230 11.05836 10.905379 9.431124   3.373235
## 5 0.5891882 10.25983 10.72588  9.513321 8.839018   3.507444
##  [ reached 'max' / getOption("max.print") -- omitted 5 rows ]
# Identify numeric gene columns in the log-transformed data
gene_names <- colnames(lgg_data_log2)[sapply(lgg_data_log2, is.numeric)]

# Calculate variance per gene on the log scale
gene_vars <- apply(lgg_data_log2[, gene_names], 2, var)

# Filter out the bottom 20% lowest variance genes
threshold <- quantile(gene_vars, 0.2)
high_var_genes <- names(gene_vars[gene_vars > threshold])

# Create filtered dataset
lgg_data_filtered <- lgg_data_log2[, c("SUBTYPE", high_var_genes)]

# Report dropping results and remaining counts
genes_dropped <- length(gene_names) - length(high_var_genes)
genes_remaining <- length(high_var_genes)

cat("Summary of Variance Filtering:\n")
## Summary of Variance Filtering:
cat("- Number of genes dropped (low variance):", genes_dropped, "\n")
## - Number of genes dropped (low variance): 3729
cat("- Number of genes remaining (high variance):", genes_remaining, "\n")
## - Number of genes remaining (high variance): 14912
# Display first 10 rows (limited columns for readability)
head(lgg_data_filtered[, 1:5], 10)
##                 SUBTYPE UBE2Q2P2  HMGB1P1  RNU12.2P    EZHIP
## 1  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 4.569995 8.074548 0.5054845 1.631244
## 2             LGG_IDHwt 4.470973 7.300920 1.4464150 2.154032
## 3  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 3.587869 7.540229 0.0000000 1.448954
## 4  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 2.604902 8.081276 0.4071896 1.204329
## 5  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 4.966292 7.763817 0.0000000 2.009383
## 6      LGG_IDHmut-codel 4.852428 7.328756 1.8302148 3.287339
## 7  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 3.281402 7.637987 0.8535167 1.935196
## 8  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 4.039717 7.711089 0.7390704 1.588517
## 9             LGG_IDHwt 3.784253 8.001622 0.0000000 0.000000
## 10     LGG_IDHmut-codel 4.388203 7.030325 0.0000000 1.911078
# 1. Identify all genes that were NOT kept (variance <= threshold)
dropped_gene_names <- names(gene_vars[gene_vars <= threshold])

# 2. Extract full data for ALL dropped genes (including SUBTYPE)
# This creates a dataframe with all 6000+ dropped columns
lgg_data_dropped_full <- lgg_data_log2[, c("SUBTYPE", dropped_gene_names)]

# 3. Accessing the data:
# To see the full list in RStudio, use: View(lgg_data_dropped_full)
cat("Full dataset of dropped genes created with", length(dropped_gene_names), "columns.\n")
## Full dataset of dropped genes created with 3729 columns.
# 1. Create a summary table of the dropped genes and their variances
dropped_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = dropped_gene_names,
  Variance = gene_vars[dropped_gene_names]
)

# 2. Show the Top 10 genes with the absolute lowest variance
cat("\n--- Top 10 Genes with the Lowest Variance (Most Stable) ---\n")
## 
## --- Top 10 Genes with the Lowest Variance (Most Stable) ---
# Sorting by variance in ascending order
top_10_low_var <- dropped_summary[order(dropped_summary$Variance), ]
print(head(top_10_low_var, 10))
##                  Gene    Variance
## ERBB2_log2 ERBB2_log2 0.008709356
## CIAO1           CIAO1 0.028175395
## TRPC4AP       TRPC4AP 0.034116398
## GGNBP2         GGNBP2 0.038112914
## GPBP1           GPBP1 0.042281663
## KCMF1           KCMF1 0.042554881
## PI4KB           PI4KB 0.043193463
## ZDHHC7         ZDHHC7 0.043753594
## UBE2D2         UBE2D2 0.044652696
## UBE2V1         UBE2V1 0.044870544
# 3. Calculate and show the Variance Range of all dropped genes
var_range <- range(dropped_summary$Variance)
cat("\n--- Variance Range of All Dropped Genes ---\n")
## 
## --- Variance Range of All Dropped Genes ---
cat("Minimum Variance:", var_range[1], "\n")
## Minimum Variance: 0.008709356
cat("Maximum Variance (at 20% threshold):", var_range[2], "\n")
## Maximum Variance (at 20% threshold): 0.1744221
# Standardize only the remaining high-variance genes
num_cols_final <- sapply(lgg_data_filtered, is.numeric)
lgg_data_std <- lgg_data_filtered
lgg_data_std[, num_cols_final] <- scale(lgg_data_std[, num_cols_final])

# Display finalized preprocessed data
head(lgg_data_std[, 1:5], 10)
##                 SUBTYPE   UBE2Q2P2    HMGB1P1   RNU12.2P      EZHIP
## 1  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel  0.7896837  1.9055436 -0.6830402 -0.7534410
## 2             LGG_IDHwt  0.6740512  0.2673209  0.7812778 -0.2036421
## 3  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel -0.3571843  0.7740775 -1.4696976 -0.9451495
## 4  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel -1.5050345  1.9197896 -0.8360111 -1.2024137
## 5  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel  1.2524551  1.2475432 -1.4696976 -0.3557648
## 6      LGG_IDHmut-codel  1.1194920  0.3262655  1.3785640  0.9882191
## 7  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel -0.7150586  0.9810885 -0.1414170 -0.4337852
## 8  LGG_IDHmut-non-codel  0.1704572  1.1358877 -0.3195234 -0.7983758
## 9             LGG_IDHwt -0.1278592  1.7511164 -1.4696976 -2.4689658
## 10     LGG_IDHmut-codel  0.5773980 -0.3056864 -1.4696976 -0.4591490
# Task 8: Save preprocessed and filtered data
write.csv(lgg_data_filtered, "../data/lgg_mrna_preprocessed_highvar.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Preprocessed data saved to lgg_mrna_preprocessed_highvar.csv\n")
## Preprocessed data saved to lgg_mrna_preprocessed_highvar.csv
# Task 9 Training & Testing Split


# set randon seed
set.seed(2026)

# Calculate the number of training samples (80%)
train_idx <- sample(1:nrow(lgg_data_std), 0.8 * nrow(lgg_data_std))

# Create training and testing datasets
train_data <- lgg_data_std[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- lgg_data_std[-train_idx, ]

# Verify the split
cat("Training set size:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
## Training set size: 405
cat("Testing set size:", nrow(test_data), "\n")
## Testing set size: 102
# Run PCA
# PCA can condense the differences of thousands of genes into several major "components" (PC1, PC2... ) 。 We plotted the scatter plot of the first two principal components and colored them according to the tumor subtypes (SUBTYPE) to see if the different tumors could be clearly separated after dimensionality reduction.

# 1. Run PCA on numeric columns of the training data
# We exclude the first column 'SUBTYPE' from the calculation
pca_res <- prcomp(train_data[, -1], center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE) 

# 2. Extract PC scores for plotting
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x)
pca_df$SUBTYPE <- train_data$SUBTYPE

# 3. Plot PC1 vs PC2
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = SUBTYPE, fill = SUBTYPE)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.95, geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA of LGG mRNA Expression (Training Set)",
       x = "Principal Component 1",
       y = "Principal Component 2")

# Task 11 Scree Plot

# Calculate variance explained by each principal component
prop_var <- (pca_res$sdev^2) / sum(pca_res$sdev^2)

# Create a data frame for the first 25 PCs
scree_df <- data.frame(PC = 1:25, Variance = prop_var[1:25])

# Plot the Scree Plot
ggplot(scree_df, aes(x = PC, y = Variance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(group = 1, color = "red") +
  geom_point(color = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot of First 25 Principal Components",
       x = "Principal Component", y = "Proportion of Variance Explained")

# Calculate cumulative variance explained by the first 25 PCs
cum_var_25 <- sum(prop_var[1:25])
cat("Total variance explained by the first 25 PCs:", round(cum_var_25 * 100, 2), "%\n")
## Total variance explained by the first 25 PCs: 65.46 %
# Combine SUBTYPE with the PC scores
pca_final_output <- cbind(SUBTYPE = train_data$SUBTYPE, as.data.frame(pca_res$x))

# Write the dimension-reduced data (PC scores) to CSV
write.csv(pca_final_output, "lgg_pca_reduced_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Dimension reduced data has been saved successfully.\n")
## Dimension reduced data has been saved successfully.

Analytical Question

Assessing Whether Multinomial Logistic Regression

Outperforms Random Assignment in Predicting

Molecular Subtypes of Brain Lower-Grade Gliomas

# --- Task 3: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) on Training Data ---
# Compute PCs using training set features to avoid information leakage
pca_train_tibble <- as_tibble(pca_res$x)
print(head(pca_train_tibble, 10))
## # A tibble: 10 × 405
##       PC1    PC2    PC3    PC4    PC5    PC6    PC7    PC8      PC9    PC10

##  2  70.1   56.8  -33.7    8.00   8.96 -35.9    3.21  -1.61  -9.93    50.9  
##  3 -41.5  -52.2   31.4   -4.26  11.2  -34.5   -9.26   3.44   9.77   -14.5  
##  4  12.3   13.3  -91.0   22.1  -16.9   30.4  -36.9  -17.6  -22.1     -3.11 
##  5  31.1   -9.65  30.8  -30.1   -4.44   7.44 -11.9    1.68   5.68    -4.29 
##  6  -5.70  26.8  -82.3   32.3   -5.61  21.4   27.0    4.68  -6.45   -15.0  
##  7  -5.01 -10.6   22.5  -23.7   14.8   11.8   20.0  -11.0   -3.37    -1.91 
##  8 -75.8    9.66  31.7   43.9   40.5   15.8   10.4   -4.76   0.0311   0.198
##  9  30.8   39.2   35.7   18.8  -14.3   10.4  -39.4   -4.22 -12.9      3.47 
## 10  -3.62  20.1   29.9   42.3  -57.0   -7.63   1.39  -8.86  11.7     -4.38 
## # ℹ 395 more variables: PC11 <dbl>, PC12 <dbl>, PC13 <dbl>, PC14 <dbl>,
## #   PC15 <dbl>, PC16 <dbl>, PC17 <dbl>, PC18 <dbl>, PC19 <dbl>, PC20 <dbl>,
## #   PC21 <dbl>, PC22 <dbl>, PC23 <dbl>, PC24 <dbl>, PC25 <dbl>, PC26 <dbl>,
## #   PC27 <dbl>, PC28 <dbl>, PC29 <dbl>, PC30 <dbl>, PC31 <dbl>, PC32 <dbl>,
## #   PC33 <dbl>, PC34 <dbl>, PC35 <dbl>, PC36 <dbl>, PC37 <dbl>, PC38 <dbl>,
## #   PC39 <dbl>, PC40 <dbl>, PC41 <dbl>, PC42 <dbl>, PC43 <dbl>, PC44 <dbl>,
## #   PC45 <dbl>, PC46 <dbl>, PC47 <dbl>, PC48 <dbl>, PC49 <dbl>, PC50 <dbl>, …
# --- Task 4: Project Test Data onto Training PCA Space ---
# Apply the rotation matrix learned from training data to the test set
test_pca_scores <- predict(pca_res, newdata = test_data[, -1])

pca_test_tibble <- as_tibble(test_pca_scores)
print(head(pca_test_tibble, 10))
## # A tibble: 10 × 405
##       PC1    PC2     PC3    PC4    PC5     PC6     PC7    PC8     PC9    PC10
##     <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1  26.6  -22.4  -19.3   -45.0   69.7   32.5    33.0    -5.97   0.394  -0.523
##  2 -16.9   23.7   -0.758 -26.5   -7.37  21.0    -8.15   18.0    3.94   -7.64 
##  3   8.06  28.1   55.3     3.02  11.0   41.7     5.31  -10.7    5.57  -16.3  
##  4 -11.8    9.29 -77.4    -1.60  -3.53  36.3    -0.153   8.06 -14.3     1.71 
##  5 -13.6   -9.22  21.4    -9.67 -20.8    6.78    3.13   -4.51 -22.4     4.26 
##  6 -10.6  -43.8   -2.65    2.18  27.6  -10.9    -9.52   10.00  -9.07   -5.98 
##  7 -10.4   33.8   -1.10  -11.4  -13.7   15.9    -0.605 -16.3   -1.34    0.850
##  8  19.9  -35.2   29.2   -51.6  -10.5   -1.000   8.11   -6.24  -0.535 -16.1  
##  9 -35.2   -2.38 -39.2     7.05   5.29   5.07   -7.03    7.00   0.334  -2.20 
## 10 -27.6   22.4  -60.0   -15.1   -8.25  10.4   -14.6    18.3   11.0    -6.99 
## # ℹ 395 more variables: PC11 <dbl>, PC12 <dbl>, PC13 <dbl>, PC14 <dbl>,
## #   PC15 <dbl>, PC16 <dbl>, PC17 <dbl>, PC18 <dbl>, PC19 <dbl>, PC20 <dbl>,
## #   PC21 <dbl>, PC22 <dbl>, PC23 <dbl>, PC24 <dbl>, PC25 <dbl>, PC26 <dbl>,
## #   PC27 <dbl>, PC28 <dbl>, PC29 <dbl>, PC30 <dbl>, PC31 <dbl>, PC32 <dbl>,
## #   PC33 <dbl>, PC34 <dbl>, PC35 <dbl>, PC36 <dbl>, PC37 <dbl>, PC38 <dbl>,
## #   PC39 <dbl>, PC40 <dbl>, PC41 <dbl>, PC42 <dbl>, PC43 <dbl>, PC44 <dbl>,
## #   PC45 <dbl>, PC46 <dbl>, PC47 <dbl>, PC48 <dbl>, PC49 <dbl>, PC50 <dbl>, …
# --- Task 6: Analyze Molecular Subtype Distribution ---
# Check for class imbalance in both training and testing sets
train_counts <- table(train_data$SUBTYPE)
test_counts <- table(test_data$SUBTYPE)

cat("--- Original Training Counts ---\n")
## --- Original Training Counts ---
print(train_counts)
## 
##     LGG_IDHmut-codel LGG_IDHmut-non-codel            LGG_IDHwt 
##                  135                  197                   73
— Task 8: Recommendation based on ‘One in Ten Rule’ —
Justification: The smallest class (LGG_IDHwt) has 73 samples.
73 / 10 = 7.3, supporting the use of 7 Principal Components. 
# --- Task 7: Address Class Imbalance via Up-sampling ---
library(caret)
## Warning: package 'caret' was built under R version 4.5.3
## Loading required package: lattice
set.seed(2026)

# Select top X PCs based on the 'One in Ten' rule determined in Task 8
X_features <- 7
pc_names <- paste0("PC", 1:X_features)

# Integrate SUBTYPE labels with PCA scores for the training set
train_pca_data <- cbind(SUBTYPE = train_data$SUBTYPE, as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:X_features]))

# Perform up-sampling to balance the classes
train_balanced <- upSample(x = train_pca_data[, pc_names], 
                           y = as.factor(train_pca_data$SUBTYPE), 
                           yname = "SUBTYPE")

cat("\n--- Balanced Training Counts (Up-sampled) ---\n")
## 
## --- Balanced Training Counts (Up-sampled) ---
print(table(train_balanced$SUBTYPE))
## 
##     LGG_IDHmut-codel LGG_IDHmut-non-codel            LGG_IDHwt 
##                  197                  197                  197
# --- Task 9: Fit Multinomial Logistic Regression Model ---
library(nnet)

# Ensure SUBTYPE is a factor and set 'LGG_IDHwt' as the clinical reference level
# Observe the biological differences between the other two subtypes (IDHmut) and this most dangerous subtype
train_balanced$SUBTYPE <- as.factor(train_balanced$SUBTYPE)
train_balanced$SUBTYPE <- relevel(train_balanced$SUBTYPE, ref = "LGG_IDHwt")

# Define the model formula
model_formula <- as.formula(paste("SUBTYPE ~", paste(pc_names, collapse = " + ")))

# Fit the model using the balanced training data
model_lgg <- multinom(model_formula, data = train_balanced, trace = FALSE)

# Display model parameters and fit statistics
cat("\n--- Multinomial Model Summary ---\n")
## 
## --- Multinomial Model Summary ---
summary(model_lgg)
## Call:
## multinom(formula = model_formula, data = train_balanced, trace = FALSE)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      (Intercept)         PC1         PC2        PC3         PC4
## LGG_IDHmut-codel      -0.8660547 -0.12837799 -0.04546807 0.02012520 -0.02605851
## LGG_IDHmut-non-codel   2.6818359 -0.05525061 -0.03131976 0.09920972 -0.20953363
##                             PC5         PC6         PC7
## LGG_IDHmut-codel      0.1079751 -0.02598066 -0.07106614
## LGG_IDHmut-non-codel -0.0909236  0.18315975 -0.09178412
## 
## Std. Errors:
##                      (Intercept)        PC1        PC2        PC3        PC4
## LGG_IDHmut-codel       1.9005088 0.02765698 0.01509762 0.02819204 0.04876800
## LGG_IDHmut-non-codel   0.9543336 0.01851892 0.01141522 0.02151747 0.05069631
##                             PC5        PC6        PC7
## LGG_IDHmut-codel     0.04119431 0.05492760 0.03790862
## LGG_IDHmut-non-codel 0.02925107 0.04827349 0.02690914
## 
## Residual Deviance: 98.92731 
## AIC: 130.9273
# --- Task 10: Ensure factor levels are aligned ---

# 1. Get the true labels from the test set
actual_labels <- as.factor(test_data$SUBTYPE)

# 2. Get the model's predicted labels
# Key: Force the predicted factor levels to exactly match the true labels' levels
test_pred_7 <- predict(model_lgg, newdata = pca_test_tibble)
test_pred_7 <- factor(test_pred_7, levels = levels(actual_labels))

# 3. Generate the confusion matrix
conf_matrix_7 <- table(Predicted = test_pred_7, Actual = actual_labels)

cat("--------------------- Confusion Matrix (7 PCs) ---------------------\n")
## --------------------- Confusion Matrix (7 PCs) ---------------------
print(conf_matrix_7)
##                       Actual
## Predicted              LGG_IDHmut-codel LGG_IDHmut-non-codel LGG_IDHwt
##   LGG_IDHmut-codel                   32                    1         0
##   LGG_IDHmut-non-codel                0                   48         0
##   LGG_IDHwt                           0                    2        19
# 4. Recalculate the misclassification rate
accuracy <- sum(diag(conf_matrix_7)) / sum(conf_matrix_7)
miss_rate_7 <- 1 - accuracy

cat("\n Misclassification Rate (7 PCs):", round(miss_rate_7, 4), "\n")
## 
##  Misclassification Rate (7 PCs): 0.0294
# --- Task 11: Training and Validating 2-PC Model ---

# 1. Define feature set (PC1, PC2)
X2 <- 2
pc_names_2 <- paste0("PC", 1:X2)

# 2. Fit the model using the balanced training data (already created in Task 7)
formula_2 <- as.formula(paste("SUBTYPE ~", paste(pc_names_2, collapse = " + ")))
model_2pc <- multinom(formula_2, data = train_balanced, trace = FALSE)

# 3. Project Test Data (only first 2 PCs)
test_pc_2 <- as.data.frame(predict(pca_res, newdata = test_data[, -1])[, 1:X2])

# 4. Predict and Align Levels
pred_2pc <- predict(model_2pc, newdata = test_pc_2)
pred_2pc <- factor(pred_2pc, levels = levels(as.factor(test_data$SUBTYPE)))

# 5. Calculate Misclassification Rate
conf_matrix_2 <- table(Predicted = pred_2pc, Actual = test_data$SUBTYPE)
miss_rate_2 <- 1 - sum(diag(conf_matrix_2)) / sum(conf_matrix_2)

cat("--- Task 11 Result ---\n")
## --- Task 11 Result ---
cat("Misclassification Rate (2 PCs):", round(miss_rate_2, 4), "\n")
## Misclassification Rate (2 PCs): 0.2157
# --- Task 12: Training and Validating 100-PC Model ---

# 1. Prepare Training Data with 100 PCs
X100 <- 100
pc_names_100 <- paste0("PC", 1:X100)
train_pca_100 <- cbind(SUBTYPE = train_data$SUBTYPE, 
                       as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:X100]))

# 2. Re-balance (upSample) for 100 features
set.seed(2026)
train_bal_100 <- upSample(x = train_pca_100[, pc_names_100], 
                          y = as.factor(train_pca_100$SUBTYPE), 
                          yname = "SUBTYPE")

# 3. Fit the model
formula_100 <- as.formula(paste("SUBTYPE ~", paste(pc_names_100, collapse = " + ")))
model_100pc <- multinom(formula_100, data = train_bal_100, trace = FALSE)

# 4. Project Test Data (first 100 PCs)
test_pc_100 <- as.data.frame(predict(pca_res, newdata = test_data[, -1])[, 1:X100])

# 5. Predict and Align Levels
pred_100pc <- predict(model_100pc, newdata = test_pc_100)
pred_100pc <- factor(pred_100pc, levels = levels(as.factor(test_data$SUBTYPE)))

# 6. Calculate Misclassification Rate
conf_matrix_100 <- table(Predicted = pred_100pc, Actual = test_data$SUBTYPE)
miss_rate_100 <- 1 - sum(diag(conf_matrix_100)) / sum(conf_matrix_100)

cat("--- Task 12 Result ---\n")
## --- Task 12 Result ---
cat("Misclassification Rate (100 PCs):", round(miss_rate_100, 4), "\n")
## Misclassification Rate (100 PCs): 0.0196
